metadata.dataset_name: Situational Rights Taxonomy (SRT) - Brazil Police Encounters
statutes_covered: Federal Constitution of Brazil (Constituição Federal), Article 5
Code of Criminal Procedure (Código de Processo Penal, CPP)
Brazilian Traffic Code (Código de Trânsito Brasileiro, CTB)
Statute of the Child and Adolescent (Estatuto da Criança e do Adolescente, ECA)
Statute of Racial Equality (Estatuto da Igualdade Racial, Lei 12.288/2010)
metadata.disclaimer: General legal information only, based on the formal text of Brazilian federal law. Local implementation and enforcement can vary, and formal legal protections are not always applied consistently in practice, particularly around racial profiling, which is well documented in Brazil. Always verify with a licensed Brazilian lawyer (advogado) or the Defensoria Pública. All entries require attorney review before use.
metadata.project: The Law in Your Language - CivilianIQ

entries.situation_description: Police stop you and ask to check your identity documents
entries.right_plain: You are generally expected to be able to identify yourself when lawfully asked by police, though Brazilian law does not require carrying an ID document on you at all times in every situation. You can ask the officer to identify themselves and state the reason for the approach.
entries.script: Boa tarde, pode se identificar e explicar o motivo da abordagem? Aqui estão meus documentos.
escalation: Ask the officer to identify themselves
Ask for the specific reason for the approach
Comply by showing identification if you have it
Note the officer's name, badge number (matrícula), and unit
If you believe the approach was unlawful or discriminatory, you can file a complaint with the Corregedoria or Ouvidoria da Polícia
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not refuse to identify yourself once a lawful reason is given. Do not physically resist even if you disagree — raise concerns afterward through a complaint.

entries.situation_description: Traffic police stop your vehicle at a checkpoint (blitz) or individually
entries.right_plain: You must show your driver's licence (CNH) and vehicle registration/licensing document (CRLV) when asked during a lawful traffic stop. You are entitled to know the reason for the stop.
entries.script: Boa tarde, aqui está minha CNH e o CRLV. Qual é o motivo da abordagem?
escalation: Provide the required documents calmly
Ask the specific reason for the stop if unclear
Note the officer's details if you plan to dispute a fine (multa)
Traffic fines can generally be formally contested (recurso) with the traffic authority (DETRAN) within a set deadline
Keep a copy or photo of any ticket (auto de infração) issued
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not argue extensively at the roadside about a disputed fine — contest it through the proper administrative process afterward. Do not drive away before being told you may leave.

entries.situation_description: Police want to search your vehicle or phone during a traffic stop
entries.right_plain: Police generally need reasonable grounds (fundada suspeita) to search a vehicle without consent, such as visible evidence of a crime or matching a specific alert. Searching the contents of a phone generally requires a stronger legal basis, similar to a home search, given constitutional protections for private communications.
entries.right_formal: Code of Criminal Procedure Art. 240-244; Federal Constitution Art. 5, XII (secrecy of communications)
entries.script: Qual é a suspeita fundada para essa revista? Eu não autorizo a busca no meu celular sem uma justificativa legal clara.
escalation: Ask for the specific grounds for the search
You can state that you do not consent, while not physically resisting
Note the officers' details and the time and place
Consult a lawyer or the Defensoria Pública afterward if you believe the search was unlawful
An unlawful search can be challenged in court and may affect the admissibility of evidence found
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not physically block officers from searching if they proceed anyway — object verbally and challenge it afterward through a lawyer.

entries.situation_description: You are placed under arrest (voz de prisão)
entries.right_plain: No one can be arrested except when caught in the act of committing a crime (flagrante delito) or by a written and reasoned order from a judicial authority, except in cases of military crime or crimes defined by military justice as specified by the Constitution.
entries.script: Por favor, informe qual é a fundamentação da minha prisão — é em flagrante ou há um mandado judicial?
escalation: Ask for the specific legal basis for the arrest
Ask to see a warrant if one is claimed to exist
Ask that your family and a lawyer be notified
Note the time of arrest — this matters for the custody hearing deadline
Contact a lawyer or the Defensoria Pública immediately
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not physically resist arrest even if you believe it is unlawful — challenge it through the legal process instead, including at the custody hearing.

entries.situation_description: You have just been arrested and want to know your immediate rights
entries.right_plain: The arrest of any person must be immediately communicated to a competent judge and to the family of the arrested person, or to a person indicated by them. An arrested person has the right to identify those responsible for their arrest or interrogation, and the right to remain silent, with assistance of family and a lawyer guaranteed.
entries.right_formal: Federal Constitution Art. 5, LXII and LXIII
entries.script: Quero permanecer em silêncio e exercer meu direito de comunicar minha prisão à minha família e a um advogado.
escalation: State clearly that you are exercising your right to remain silent
Ask that your family and a lawyer be notified immediately
Ask for the names and roles of the officers involved in the arrest
Do not sign any statement without a lawyer reviewing it
A custody hearing (audiência de custódia) must generally occur within 24 hours
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not answer questions before a lawyer is present if you can avoid it. Do not sign documents you have not read and understood.

entries.situation_description: You have been arrested and are waiting to be presented to a judge
entries.right_plain: Following an arrest, the person must generally be brought before a judge within 24 hours for a custody hearing (audiência de custódia), where the judge reviews the legality of the arrest and decides whether to convert it to preventive detention, apply alternative measures, or order release.
entries.right_formal: Code of Criminal Procedure Art. 310; National Council of Justice Resolution 213/2015
entries.script: Gostaria de saber quando será a audiência de custódia e se meu advogado poderá estar presente.
escalation: Ask when the custody hearing is scheduled
Ensure your lawyer or a public defender is notified and can attend
At the hearing, any mistreatment during arrest should be reported to the judge
If the 24-hour deadline is exceeded without justification, this is a serious issue a lawyer should raise immediately
The judge can order release, alternative measures, or, in some cases, continued detention — each of these can be appealed
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not waive the custody hearing without understanding what you are giving up — consult a lawyer first if at all possible.

entries.situation_description: Police want to enter or search your home
entries.right_plain: The home is an inviolable asylum. No one may enter without the resident's consent, except in cases of flagrante delicto or disaster, to provide help, or, during the day, by judicial order (mandado de busca e apreensão). At night, entry without consent is only allowed for flagrante delicto, disaster, or to render aid.
entries.script: Vocês têm um mandado judicial de busca e apreensão? Posso ver o documento antes de autorizar a entrada.
escalation: Ask to see the judicial warrant before allowing entry
Note whether it is day or night — this affects what is legally permitted without a warrant
Note the names of officers present and any witnesses
Ask for an inventory of anything taken
Contact a lawyer immediately afterward — an unlawful entry can be challenged in court
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not physically obstruct officers even if you believe the entry is unlawful — object verbally and challenge it afterward through a lawyer.

entries.situation_description: Police want to conduct a personal/body search
entries.right_plain: A personal search generally requires a founded suspicion (fundada suspeita) that the person is carrying a weapon or evidence of a crime — it should not be arbitrary. Personal searches should be conducted by an officer of the same sex where reasonably possible.
entries.script: Qual é a suspeita fundada para essa revista pessoal?
escalation: Ask for the specific grounds for the search
Note the officers' names and the time/location
If you believe the search was arbitrary or discriminatory, this can be raised in a complaint
Consult a lawyer or the Defensoria Pública afterward
Document the experience as soon as possible while details are fresh
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not physically resist a search being conducted by police — object verbally and follow up through the proper channels afterward.

entries.situation_description: You or a family member wants to seek release from custody
entries.right_plain: For many offences, release on bail (fiança) is possible, sometimes set directly by the police delegado for minor offences, otherwise by a judge. Separately, habeas corpus is a constitutional remedy anyone can request — including without a lawyer, in principle — whenever someone suffers or is threatened with unlawful restraint on their freedom of movement.
entries.right_formal: Federal Constitution Art. 5, LXVI, LXVIII; Code of Criminal Procedure Art. 322-350
entries.script: Gostaria de saber se cabe fiança neste caso, e gostaria que meu advogado avaliasse a possibilidade de um habeas corpus.
escalation: Ask whether bail is available for the specific offence
Have a lawyer evaluate whether a habeas corpus petition is appropriate
Gather documentation supporting release (stable residence, employment, family ties)
Understand and follow any conditions attached to release strictly
Stay in contact with your lawyer as the case proceeds
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not violate any conditions of release. Do not assume release means the case is over — it is not an acquittal.

entries.situation_description: Police are questioning you and you want to know if you must answer
entries.right_plain: You have a constitutional right to remain silent and not produce evidence against yourself. This applies from the moment of any police approach through formal interrogation. Silence cannot be used against you as an admission of guilt.
entries.script: Quero exercer meu direito constitucional de permanecer calado até falar com um advogado.
escalation: State clearly that you are invoking your right to remain silent
Ask for a lawyer before answering further questions
Do not sign a statement (depoimento) without a lawyer reviewing it
This right applies at every stage, not just formal interrogation
Contact the Defensoria Pública if you cannot afford a lawyer
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not assume you must answer 'just a few questions' — the right applies from the first approach. Do not sign anything you have not read and understood.

entries.situation_description: You or a family member wants legal representation
entries.right_plain: Everyone has the right to a lawyer at every stage of a criminal process. If you cannot afford one, the state provides free legal assistance through the Defensoria Pública, which is a full public institution dedicated to this, not just an appointed private lawyer.
entries.right_formal: Federal Constitution Art. 5, LXXIV; Art. 134 (Defensoria Pública)
entries.script: Não posso pagar um advogado particular. Gostaria de ser atendido pela Defensoria Pública.
escalation: Ask to be assisted by the Defensoria Pública if you cannot afford a private lawyer
Ask for the contact information of the nearest Defensoria Pública office
Family can retain a private lawyer at any point, alongside or instead of a public defender
Keep any lawyer's contact information and case number for follow-up
The Defensoria Pública also assists with civil, family, and other legal matters, not just criminal cases
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not proceed with important questioning without legal representation if it can be avoided. Do not assume you must afford a private lawyer — the Defensoria Pública is a genuine right, not charity.

entries.situation_description: You want to file a complaint about police misconduct
entries.right_plain: Complaints about police conduct can be filed with the police force's internal affairs unit (Corregedoria), the Public Prosecutor's Office (Ministério Público), or the state/municipal Ombudsman's Office (Ouvidoria). The Abuse of Authority Law also creates specific criminal offences for police misconduct.
entries.script: Quero registrar uma denúncia sobre a conduta de um policial. Podem me orientar sobre o procedimento?
escalation: File a written complaint with the Corregedoria of the relevant police force
File a complaint with the Ministério Público, which oversees police legality
Use the Ouvidoria (Ombudsman) of the state public security department if available
Keep detailed written records, dates, and any evidence (photos, videos, witness contacts)
A lawyer or human rights organization can help draft and route a complaint for serious cases
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not delay filing — gather evidence and file while details are fresh. Do not rely on a single channel; documenting through multiple official channels can help.

entries.situation_description: You experienced or witnessed what seemed like excessive use of force by police
entries.right_plain: Police may only use force proportional to the actual resistance or threat presented, and excessive or abusive force can constitute a crime under the Abuse of Authority Law, in addition to giving rise to a civil claim against the state for damages.
entries.right_formal: Abuse of Authority Law (Lei 13.869/2019); Federal Constitution Art. 37, §6 (state civil liability)
entries.script: Preciso registrar o uso de força pelo policial e buscar atendimento médico e jurídico.
escalation: Seek medical attention and document injuries immediately with photos and medical records
Get contact details of any witnesses
File a complaint with the Corregedoria, Ministério Público, or Ouvidoria
Consult a lawyer or the Defensoria Pública about a civil claim against the state
Report the incident to a human rights organization if the case is serious
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not delay medical treatment or documentation. Do not discard damaged clothing or other physical evidence.

entries.situation_description: You want to record a police interaction, or an officer is telling you to stop
entries.right_plain: Recording police performing their public duties in a public place is generally understood to be protected by the constitutional rights to free expression and information, and several state laws and municipal ordinances have explicitly reinforced this right, particularly for recording police approaches. You should not physically interfere with the officer's work while recording.
entries.right_formal: Federal Constitution Art. 5, IV, IX, XIV (expression, information); various state 'right to record' laws
entries.script: Estou apenas registrando a abordagem, sem interferir no seu trabalho, o que é meu direito.
escalation: Continue recording calmly from a safe, non-interfering distance
State that you have a legal right to record
Do not delete footage even if asked
If your device is taken, ask for a receipt and note officer details
Contact a lawyer, the Defensoria Pública, or a digital rights organization if your rights were violated
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not physically get in the officer's way while recording. Do not delete your recording under pressure.

entries.situation_description: A minor (under 18) is stopped, questioned, or apprehended by police
entries.right_plain: Minors are subject to a distinct system (apreensão, not prisão, for those under 18) under the Statute of the Child and Adolescent, with an emphasis on socio-educational rather than punitive measures. A parent, guardian, or responsible adult should generally be notified immediately, and the Conselho Tutelar (Guardianship Council) may also be involved.
entries.right_formal: Statute of the Child and Adolescent (ECA), Law 8.069/1990, Art. 106-109
entries.script: Este é um adolescente. Peço que os responsáveis e o Conselho Tutelar sejam notificados imediatamente.
escalation: Insist that a parent/guardian be notified immediately
Ask that the Conselho Tutelar be involved as appropriate
Retain a lawyer experienced in juvenile matters (Vara da Infância e Juventude) as early as possible
Ask for the specific legal basis for any apprehension
Keep the guardian informed and involved at every stage
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not allow a minor to be questioned alone without a guardian present if it can be avoided. Do not let a minor sign any statement without a guardian and, where possible, a lawyer.
source_statutes: Statute of the Child and Adolescent Art. 106-109

entries.situation_description: You are participating in a peaceful protest or public demonstration
entries.right_plain: All persons may hold peaceful meetings in public places, without needing prior authorization, as long as they do not frustrate another meeting previously called for the same place, with prior notice to the competent authority required only so it can be informed, not so it can approve or deny the gathering.
entries.script: Esta é uma manifestação pacífica, protegida pelo artigo 5º, inciso XVI da Constituição.
escalation: Stay peaceful and avoid physical confrontation
Know that prior notice, where practical, strengthens your legal position but authorization is not required for a peaceful gathering
Document the situation safely if possible
Note officer badge numbers and any use of force
Contact a lawyer, the Defensoria Pública, or a human rights organization if your rights were violated
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not engage in violence or property destruction — this can change the legal character of the gathering. Do not ignore a clear, lawful order in a genuine emergency situation.

entries.situation_description: You believe you were stopped, searched, or treated differently by police because of your race
entries.right_plain: Racial discrimination is constitutionally prohibited and criminalized under Brazilian law, and racially discriminatory policing is a well-documented, serious concern that human rights organizations and researchers have extensively studied in Brazil. You have the right to file a complaint specifically citing racial discrimination, which can be pursued alongside any other complaint about the encounter.
entries.right_formal: Federal Constitution Art. 5 (equality); Statute of Racial Equality (Lei 12.288/2010); Law 7.716/1989 (racism as a crime)
entries.script: Quero registrar que considero esta abordagem discriminatória, e vou buscar orientação jurídica sobre isso.
escalation: Document the encounter in as much detail as possible, including exactly what was said
Get contact details of any witnesses
File a complaint that specifically raises the racial discrimination aspect, with the Corregedoria, Ministério Público, or Ouvidoria
Consult a lawyer, the Defensoria Pública, or an organization focused on racial justice in policing
Consider whether a criminal complaint under Law 7.716/1989 is appropriate with a lawyer's guidance
entries.what_not_to_do: Do not assume this is not worth reporting — documented patterns matter for accountability even when individual cases are hard to prove. Do not physically resist during the encounter itself.
